__TR_manifesto_0__: Manifest kupujícího — Stop Trusting Seller Churn | Churn Lens
__TR_manifesto_1__: The SaaS acquisition market runs on seller-computed metrics. We're changing that. The manifesto for buyer-side due diligence: raw data, your methodology, no trust required.
__TR_manifesto_23__: It's time to run your own.
__TR_manifesto_25__: 80% of SaaS acquisitions overpay because of this gap.
__TR_manifesto_27__: The Old Opportunity vs. The New Opportunity
__TR_manifesto_29__: Trust the seller's churn number. Use a 40-point DD checklist that covers legal, financial, and operational risk but not revenue durability methodology. Negotiate price based on the CIM. Discover the real churn 4 months post-close.
__TR_manifesto_2__: Manifest kupujícího — Stop Trusting Seller Churn
__TR_manifesto_31__: Demand the raw subscription CSV. Compute churn five ways yourself. Flag concentration risk, annual-plan exposure, and zombie MRR before you close. Negotiate from data the seller can't manipulate. Know what you're buying.
__TR_manifesto_33__: The Three Principles of Buyer-Side Due Diligence
__TR_manifesto_35__: A summary metric computed by the party selling you the business is not due diligence. It's marketing. The only churn number that matters is the one you compute from the raw subscription export — customer ID, MRR, plan, status, dates. No aggregations. No filters. The raw CSV.
__TR_manifesto_37__: Logo churn alone is meaningless. Revenue churn alone is meaningless. You need both — plus net churn, gross churn, and cohort-adjusted churn — to see the full picture. When logo churn and revenue churn diverge, downgrades are hiding in the gap. One number can't show you that.
__TR_manifesto_39__: A 40-point checklist that says "ask the seller for the churn rate" is not protection — it's theater. Real due diligence is forensic: you're looking for the methodology choices that flatter the number, not trusting the number itself. Churn Lens automates that forensic analysis so every buyer can do it in minutes.
